symptoms: BUG: soft lockup - CPU#12 stuck for 67s! [xfsdatad/12:4514]
PANIC: "Kernel panic - not syncing: softlockup: hung tasks"

workflow.thought: Examine backtrace of the xfsdatad task to understand execution flow
workflow.observation: Stack trace showing _spin_unlock_irqrestore and wake_up functions

workflow.thought: Inspect wait queue structure at the address from registers
workflow.observation: Wait queue lock state and task list pointers

workflow.thought: Check which processes are currently runnable on CPU0
workflow.observation: Only idle tasks and few kernel threads on CPU0

workflow.thought: Examine runqueue state to see task scheduling
workflow.observation: CPU0 runqueue shows xfsdatad as current with watchdog queued

workflow.thought: Compare runqueue clocks to detect CPU stall
workflow.observation: CPU0 clock value for timing comparison

workflow.thought: Compare with other CPU clocks to detect stall
workflow.observation: CPU1 clock value showing time difference

workflow.thought: Calculate time difference between CPU runqueues
Count tasks stuck in io_schedule across all processes
Count nfsd tasks to identify potential source
Check if nfsd tasks are stuck in io_schedule
workflow.action: foreach bt | grep -A2 "COMMAND: \"nfsd\"" | grep io_schedule | wc -l

workflow.thought: Check module taint status for third-party drivers
Switch to watchdog task to examine its scheduling
workflow.observation: Watchdog task PID 6 in TASK_RUNNING state
Watchdog last_queued time matches runqueue clock

workflow.thought: Switch to xfsdatad task to examine its scheduling
Calculate how long xfsdatad has been on CPU

root_cause_analysis: It appears that there were a lot of tasks waiting for page writeback to complete and the xfsdatad task had to wake all of them up while holding a spinlock with interrupts disabled and it took longer than expected and triggered the soft lockup watchdog to think the CPU was stuck.
